microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's **recovery validation program** from a 2-person operation into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, production-ready model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new benchmark for continuous validation at enterprise scale.

Built an **internal AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — directly mirroring the research-to-product translation demanded in ML feature deployment.
Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**.
microsoft_2023: Championed **production adoption** of AI-driven code generation workflows, running end-to-end demos and training sessions that drove utilization across 14 key engineering teams and saved **37 engineering hours monthly**.

Shaped the product roadmap to include **intent-based generation features** by facilitating 11+ structured user interviews with Azure service teams, surfacing critical capability gaps and translating research-stage concepts into actionable requirements.

Improved data retrieval quality by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** pipeline, cutting lookup time by 83% and increasing developer adoption of LLM-powered tooling.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework spanning 140+ engineering teams.

Identified **81 hours of manual toil per region launch** through discovery interviews with 20+ service teams, then built and drove a roadmap to automate critical execution paths — directly paralleling ML feature productionization workflows.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to surface operational bottlenecks across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to prioritize high-impact automation investments and establish measurable quality benchmarks for execution.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy and mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations in a large-scale production environment.

Drove strategic alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing a repeatable release process for production-grade robotics software.

Facilitated Agile sprint planning and retrospectives, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilizing the developer intake process — improving cross-team execution velocity across a multi-org engineering organization.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing end-to-end scoping and invoicing workflows for 200+ monthly proposals through a redesigned CRM process, improving operational reliability and conversion throughput.
Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversion rate by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy including website optimization and Google profile improvements.
Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records to identify and close margin leaks across high-volume service lines.

tailoring_notes: Wove in 'production-ready' (JD term) in microsoft_ft bullet 2 — reframed the Resilience Automation Platform as a production-grade deployment model matching SambaNova's enterprise reliability focus.

Used 'research-to-product translation' (direct JD responsibility) in microsoft_ft bullet 4 and microsoft_2022 bullet 2 — tied AI agent and toil-automation work explicitly to the bridge role between ML research and product engineering.

Mirrored 'benchmarks and quality standards' (JD responsibility) in microsoft_2022 bullet 3 — reframed the Power BI dashboard outcome as establishing measurable benchmarks, matching the JD's call to 'establish benchmarks and quality standards for ML features in production environments.'
Injected 'intent-based generation features' and 'LLM-powered tooling' (JD vocabulary: function calling, structured output, developer-facing ML features) into microsoft_2023 bullets 2 and 3.
Reframed amazon_robotics CI/CD bullet to emphasize 'production-grade' software release — mirroring JD's repeated emphasis on production-ready ML solutions at enterprise scale.
